title: Vertex-transitive quantum graphs
authors: Mac Hayes, Trevor Jess, Andre Kornell, Remi Salinas Schmeis
abstract: We define a quantum graph to be vertex-transitive if the join of its automorphism group is the maximum quantum relation on its quantum vertex set, in direct analogy with the classical case. All simple quantum graphs in $M_2(\mathbb C)$ are vertex-transitive, but many simple quantum graphs in $M_3(\mathbb C)$ are not vertex-transitive. We provide a complete classification of vertex-transitive quantum graphs in $M_3(\mathbb C)$ up to isomorphism. To do this, we introduce a polynomial invariant for quantum graphs in $M_n(\mathbb C)$, which we call the panoramic polynomial.
summary: This paper rigorously classifies vertex-transitive quantum graphs, which are subspaces of matrices in $M_n(\mathbb{C})$. The authors introduce the panoramic polynomial as a key invariant and use spectral analysis of the automorphism group to prove a complete, exhaustive list of all such graphs for the $M_3(\mathbb{C})$ case. This work provides deep structural insights into the symmetries of these quantum algebraic objects.
main_problem: The primary goal is to provide a complete classification of vertex-transitive quantum graphs, with a specific focus on those embedded in $M_3(\mathbb{C})$.
main_result: The authors provide a complete classification of all vertex-transitive quantum graphs in $M_3(\mathbb{C})$ up to isomorphism, identifying several distinct families parameterized by angles $	heta$.
method: The classification relies on introducing and utilizing a polynomial invariant called the panoramic polynomial, alongside analyzing the spectrum of the adjoint action of unitaries in the automorphism group.
model_or_system: The system studied is the quantum graph, mathematically defined as a $\dagger$-closed subspace $R$ of matrices in $M_n(\mathbb{C})$, where $n$ is the dimension of the ambient space (primarily $n=3$).
key_observables: The panoramic polynomial $p_R$, the automorphism group $	ext{Aut}(R)$, the dimension $	ext{dim}(R)$, and the spectrum of the adjoint action $	ext{Spec}(	ext{Ad}_R(u))$ are key observables.
important_parameters: The dimension $n$ (especially $n=3$) and the continuous parameter $	heta \in [0, \pi/2]$ used to parameterize certain graph families are important parameters.
main_assumptions: The classification heavily assumes that the dimension of the quantum graph is an isomorphism invariant, and that the graphs under consideration are regular.
figures_summary: Figure 4 summarizes the complete classification of vertex-transitive quantum graphs in $M_3(\mathbb{C})$ modulo isomorphism, listing 8 distinct types, each characterized by its defining elements, panoramic polynomial, and automorphism group.
paper_structure: The paper builds from general definitions of quantum graphs and vertex-transitivity, introduces the panoramic polynomial invariant, proves structural theorems relating regularity and vertex-transitivity, and culminates in the explicit, exhaustive classification of all such graphs in $M_3(\mathbb{C})$ using spectral analysis.
why_interesting: While the subject is abstract algebra/noncommutative geometry, the rigorous classification of highly symmetric structures (like vertex-transitive graphs) using algebraic invariants is a powerful theoretical tool applicable to characterizing symmetries in quantum physical models.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
